source: Learning bash with Rocky - Check Your Knowledge (chapters 1-3): The book's own review quiz for the first three chapters

questions.q: Among these four shells, which one does NOT exist?
questions.why: Bash, Ksh (Korn) and Csh (C shell) exist; "Tsh" does not.

questions.q: What is the correct syntax to assign content to a variable?
questions.why: = assigns, with no spaces around it.

questions.q: How do you store the return of a command in a variable?
questions.why: $(command) (or backticks) captures a command's output; ${ls} is variable expansion and = with spaces is invalid.

questions.q: The read command allows you to read the contents of a file. True or false?
questions.why: read reads user input (from stdin), not the contents of a file.

questions.q: Which is the correct syntax for the cut command?
questions.why: -d sets the delimiter, -f the field number.

questions.q: Which command is used to shift positional variables?
questions.why: shift moves the positional variables to the left.

questions.q: Which command transforms a string into positional variables?
questions.why: set <string> splits it into positional variables.
